summary: Backend testing for ClosetAI MVP completed. All 10 pytest cases passed (100% success). Covered: auth (signup/login/me/duplicate/bad-pass/unauth), wardrobe CRUD with filter, outfit AI chat (Claude Sonnet 4.6, with seeded wardrobe and ITEM:<id> recommendations), wishlist + AI price comparison, brands (default + user-added, delete protection), events (sorted by date asc + AI suggest), friends (request/accept/access patch/list/incoming-outgoing direction), friend wardrobe access matrix (none=403, limited=only rating>=4 public/friends, full=all public/friends, private always hidden), messaging (friendship-gated, ordered, read marking, threads with unread_count, recommended_item snapshot, non-friend 403). Real JPEG base64 images (with shapes + noise) were used per /app/image_testing.md. NOTE one minor API contract deviation: review_request specified price_results items have keys `price_low/price_high` and `best_pick` flag, but server returns `estimated_price_low/estimated_price_high` (passed through from LLM JSON schema) and the per-item flag is named `is_best_pick`. Tests assert both alternatives but frontend integration should align on field names.

minor.issue: Field name mismatch vs review_request spec. Spec calls for `price_low/price_high` and `best_pick` per result; implementation returns `estimated_price_low/estimated_price_high` and `is_best_pick`. Functional but FE/BE contract should be normalised to avoid integration bugs.

Idempotent return: if a friendship already exists in either direction (pending or accepted), endpoint returns the existing friendship as 200 OK rather than 409/400. Acceptable but undocumented; consider returning a clearer status.

action_items: Decide on canonical field names for /api/wishlist/{id}/compare results (`price_low/price_high` vs `estimated_price_low/estimated_price_high`, `best_pick` flag vs `is_best_pick`) and ensure frontend mapping matches. Adjust either backend or frontend.
(Optional) Replace deprecated FastAPI @app.on_event('shutdown') with lifespan handlers in a future polish pass (functional today, no impact on tests).
(Optional) Consider validating that `image_base64` payloads are non-trivial real images server-side; current implementation accepts any string.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 943 lines and monolithic. While currently fine for MVP, recommend splitting into modules (auth, wardrobe, outfit, wishlist, brands, events, friends, messages) before adding more features to keep <700 lines per file.
MongoDB `_id` is consistently excluded via projections in find/find_one and `_doc_to_model` helpers — no ObjectId leakage observed.
All endpoints async, use Pydantic validation, raise proper HTTP status codes (401/403/404/409).
JWT bearer auth implemented correctly with bcrypt; emails lowercased on signup/login for case-insensitive matching.
AI integrations (Claude Sonnet 4.6 via emergentintegrations) wrapped with 90s timeout and 502/504 error surfacing — robust.
Friend wardrobe access logic is clean: viewer/owner asymmetric access via `access_levels[owner_id]`. Private items correctly excluded in all branches.
Messaging threads aggregation pipeline handles unread_count and partner grouping correctly.
seed_data_creation: All test data prefixed with TEST_ (users TEST_a_*, TEST_b_*, TEST_user_*, TEST_stranger_*, wardrobe items 'TEST ...', wishlist 'TEST White Sneakers', events 'TEST Wedding'/'TEST Meeting', brand 'TEST Brand X'). Some test data persists in MongoDB (no global teardown) but is clearly identifiable.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pytest file at /app/backend/tests/test_closetai_backend.py runs all 10 cases against EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL in ~33s (AI calls dominate). Real-image base64 helper in conftest.py generates JPEGs with shapes+noise. user_a/user_b session-scoped fixtures share state across messaging/friends test classes — pay attention to fixture scope if extending. The fixture `friendship` is module-scoped and modifies B->A access in TestFriends; TestFriendWardrobeAccess flips access through none/limited/full and TestMessaging assumes accepted friendship.
rca_of_issue: No production bugs found. The lone test failure during first run was a test-side bug (case mismatch); fixed by comparing against email.lower() since server normalises emails.
